what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block that records and explains an insight: the user's idea for multiple attachment scopes is already implemented as an existing scope ladder/cascade mechanism, and only needs to be extended to dragnet outputs and channels.
observations: Title claims the scopes idea is already built but not yet pointed at dragnet outputs
Source is claude_code, channel is dragnet-development, linked as part_of board://item-389c8489
Lists six scopes: channel, session, project-of-the-channel, global, working-dir, heaps
Describes an existing scope ladder: global / project / user / session
States scope is encoded as a frame in the address, e.g. decision://‹whose›/‹id›
Describes a most-specific-wins cascade: session shadows project shadows global
Mentions self-resolution via an injected self-marker and a loud failure if self cannot be determined
Frames self-resolution as a safety property preventing acting as another session
Suggests adding 'channel' and 'working-dir' as new rungs and pointing dragnet cards at the right rung
Created and updated timestamps are identical
standouts: Scope is represented as a frame inside the address URI, not a separate metadata field
The existing cascade mechanism is claimed to already cover the user's six conceptual scopes
Self-identity is injected and fails loudly rather than being guessed, treated as a safety invariant
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created, updated, history
Uses board:// URI addressing for the item and its parent link
Markdown body has a top-level heading followed by explanatory bullets
History array records a single null->current 'filed' transition
concerns: Makes strong mechanical claims about existing implementations without citing code or concrete examples in the file
Terms like 'heaps' are introduced without definition
Created and updated are identical, which is plausible for a newly filed item but leaves no edit trace
The future-dated timestamp may be fictional/stub data
notes: A noticeboard note arguing that the desired multi-scope behavior already exists as an address-frame cascade and should be wired to dragnet outputs/channels rather than rebuilt.
questions: What is the concrete code or schema for the existing scope ladder?
How would channel and working-dir scopes be encoded in the address frame?
How is the self-marker injected and validated?
What is the content and role of the linked parent item board://item-389c8489?
Are dragnet outputs currently completely unscoped, or do they use a different scoping model?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the stated purpose is to report that the scopes idea is already built and propose pointing it at dragnet outputs, and the body does exactly that.
apparent_themes: reuse existing mechanisms instead of rebuilding
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to validate the user's intuition, align the team around an existing scope mechanism, reduce perceived new work, and propose the next extension step for dragnet outputs and channels while emphasizing the safety discipline around self-resolution.
novelty: The treatment of scope as a URI address frame that simultaneously names the owner and selects the cascade rung, and the reduction of six distinct conceptual scopes into one reusable mechanism.
contribution: The concept that scope can be encoded as an address frame and resolved through a single most-specific-wins cascade across global/project/user/session/channel/working-dir scopes, plus the safety principle that self-identity must be injected and unambiguous.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item arguing that an existing address-frame scope cascade already implements the requested multi-scope behavior and only needs to be extended to dragnet outputs and channels.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate that the scopes idea is already implemented and should be wired to dragnet outputs rather than reimplemented.
